---
title: "Felix Odenthal"
subtitle: "Mechanistic interpretability research"
---

I'm an undergraduate at the University of Chicago working on mechanistic interpretability.

## Featured Work

### [The Residual Stream Has a Geometry of Time](notes/residual-geometry.qmd)

In a pilot study on Gemma-2-2B, I found a compact set of residual-stream directions with long autocorrelation lifetimes. The surprising part is geometric: these persistent directions live inside the high-variance PCA span, but not on the PCA axes themselves.

This post is the best entry point to the project. It includes the experimental setup, figures, evidence boundaries, and open questions.

[Read the post](notes/residual-geometry.qmd)

## Research Interests

- Mechanistic interpretability of transformer residual streams
- Persistent state, context tracking, and long-timescale representations
- Geometry of representation spaces: PCA, subspaces, and sparse features
- Causal tests that connect geometric structure to model behavior

## Contact

If you're interested in the residual-geometry project or related mechanistic interpretability work, the best starting point is the post above.
